title: feat(core): show first user message as thread title in chat history

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(core): generate thread title for pre-created threads
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relevance: Related — fixed title generation for pre-created threads; this PR extends the fallback behavior when generateTitle is not configured

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(core): fix TypeError in generateTitleFromUserMessage for file parts
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relevance: Related — fixed edge case in title generation from user messages; this PR adds a new code path that also extracts text from user messages

summary: When generateTitle is not configured (the default), thread history in the chat sidebar only shows timestamps, making conversations hard to identify. This PR adds a fallback that saves the user's first message (truncated to 100 chars) as the thread title. When generateTitle IS configured, existing LLM-based title generation takes priority. Also adds minor styling to make timestamps visually secondary when a title is present.
maintainerNotes: Small, well-scoped community contribution touching 2 source files (agent.ts + chat-threads.tsx) plus 2 changesets. Code logic is clean — adds an `else if` branch in agent.ts that fires only when: no generateTitle configured, no existing title, and thread doesn't already exist. CodeRabbit flagged minor nits: (1) duplicate playground-ui entries across two changeset files, (2) potential empty title if user message is only whitespace (trim produces empty string but code doesn't guard against it). No tests added — the core agent title generation area has existing test patterns that could be extended. CI checks are pending (tests haven't run, likely needs secrets/approval). No merge conflicts. No human reviewer has looked at this yet — needs maintainer review to assess whether this fallback behavior is desired as a core default and whether the empty-whitespace edge case should be guarded.
